TimeEntry.Title: BWH-HQ-APP-01, BWH-HQ-IIS-01, BWH-HQ-OPSPRB-01, RG01 BW-ACCT02, BWH-HQ-CONF-01, BWH-HQ-DT-01, BWH-HQ-DT-02, BWH-HQ-DT-03 : SNMP installation and configuration
TimeEntry.Notes: During diagnosis, I found that these machines were not sync for a long time, possibly due to recent formatting either snmp was not installed or probe string was mismatched. I installed the latest version of SNMP, configured the required SNMP thread settings, removed the old records of these machines from the portal, and then rediscovered and added them again. After checking again an hour later, all machines were properly synced with OPS Manager and were showing alerts correctly.
TimeEntry.Resource: S. Kumar Sharma CHD-TS1 Support - Remote

TimeEntry.Title: BWH-HQ-DT-04, BWH-HQ-LT-01, BWH-HQ-LT-05, BWH-HQ-LT-06, BWH-HQ-LT-07, BWH-HQ-LT-09 : SNMP installation and configuration
TimeEntry.Notes: I diagnosed the issue and found that these machines had not been syncing for a long time, possibly due to recent formatting or an upgrade to Windows 11. Additionally, SNMP was either not installed or the probe string was mismatched. I installed the latest version of SNMP, configured the required SNMP thread settings, removed the old records of these machines from the portal, and then rediscovered and added them again. After checking again an hour later, all machines were properly synced with OPS Manager and were displaying alerts correctly.
TimeEntry.Resource: S. Kumar Sharma CHD-TS1 Support - Remote

TimeEntry.Title: Nancy from BWH called in regarding the Remote App
TimeEntry.Notes:
RemoteApp Issue – Full Work Log (Chat History Summary)

As per the handover from the previous shift, the user was able to connect to the server via RDP, but published RemoteApps on the RD Farm were not launching.

Troubleshooting Performed

  1. Confirmed the issue scope

    • Direct RDP to the server was working.

    • Published RemoteApps were failing to open.

  2. Reviewed error messages

    • Observed svchost log message:

      • Attempt to access C:\Windows\system32\LogFiles\Sum\Api.chk failed with Access Denied (Error 5).

    • RemoteApp popup error seen:

      • “Network Access Protection requirements not met”

      • Error code: 0x3000027

  3. Checked RDS Collection security settings

    • Verified Session Collection security looked normal (Negotiate / Client Compatible / NLA not forced from that screen).

  4. Checked RD Gateway CAP policy

    • Opened RD Gateway Manager → Connection Authorization Policies (CAP)

    • Verified Requirements:

      • Password authentication enabled

      • Domain Users allowed

      • No explicit NAP enforcement configured in CAP

  5. Checked RD Gateway RAP policy

    • Opened RD Gateway Manager → Resource Authorization Policies (RAP)

    • Verified:

      • Domain Users allowed

      • Network resource group set to Domain Computers

      • Policy enabled

  6. Pulled RD Gateway event log detail (key finding)

    • Found log:

      • User not authorized because a tunnel could not be created

      • Auth method: NTLM

      • Protocol: HTTP

      • Error: 2147965421

    • This indicated an HTTPS tunnel/certificate problem (not actually NAP).

  7. Identified missing RD Gateway SSL certificate (root cause)

    • In RD Gateway Manager, it showed:

      • “A server certificate is not yet installed or selected”

    • Confirmed in RG01 properties → SSL Certificate:

      • Issued to/by/expiration not available (no cert bound)

  8. Verified NAP Agent status (additional check)

    • Ran:

      • sc stop napagent → service not started

      • sc config napagent start= disabled → success

    • Confirmed NAP Agent wasn’t running (certificate/tunnel was the real issue).

  9. Resolution action

    • Proceeded to create a self-signed certificate matching RD Gateway FQDN:

      • RG01.brandywinedev.local

    • Stored the root certificate as required for client trust.

    • After verifying status again, RemoteApp started working.

Outcome

? RemoteApp issue resolved after certificate-related configuration verification and status re-check.
? Confirmed RemoteApps are now launching successfully.

TimeEntry.Title: Sophos VPN not working - Nancy called
TimeEntry.Notes: Nancy reported that Sophos VPN was not working on her system. I troubleshot the issue by verifying her internet connectivity, checking the Sophos Connect client status, and reviewing VPN policy and adapter conflicts. After initial steps did not resolve the issue, I deleted the existing VPN configuration and logged into the Sophos VPN portal using her credentials to download a fresh configuration file. The new configuration was imported into Sophos Connect, after which the VPN connected successfully and was confirmed working.

TimeEntry.Title: RDP Access Issue on Nancy's System
TimeEntry.Notes:
• Verified the issue: Nancy’s system couldn’t access the RDP URL, while other systems could.

  • Network verification: Checked the network and Sophos route.

  • Found the issue: Discovered that the route was directed to the internet rather than the VPN Sophos route.

  • Resolution: Cleared the NAT entries and cleaned the host file.

  • Issue resolved: After cleaning the configurations, the RDP access issue was fixed.

TimeEntry.Title: Re: Loud fan noise and Newstar is sluggish
TimeEntry.Notes:
As reported, all servers were found to be down. Upon checking from the WinEdge machine, it was identified that the ESXi host had rebooted because auto-reboot was enabled. Once the reboot was completed, all VMs started coming up gradually.

After all servers were online, I logged into each server and verified that the services were running properly. ESXi logs indicate that the shutdown occurred due to a heat-related issue. Currently, all servers are up and performing well.
TimeEntry.Resource: S. Kumar Sharma CHD-TS1 Support - Remote

TimeEntry.Title: Re: Loud fan noise and Newstar is sluggish
TimeEntry.Notes:
As per alert connected to Win edge machine and logged into the ESXI and verified all the Vm's powered on and verified the logs and identified root causes. 

Investigated ESXi host outage where all VMs powered off and restarted via auto-start order; confirmed host-level reboot with no graceful shutdown recorded.


Reviewed ESXi events and confirmed host agent restart at ~07:55 AM PST, indicating unexpected hardware-level reset.


Logged into HP iLO and reviewed Integrated Management Log (IML) and Health Summary.


Identified multiple critical hardware errors including uncorrectable memory errors (Processor 1, DIMM 1), CPU machine check exceptions, and Smart Storage Battery failure.


Verified system entered degraded state at ~07:51–07:54 AM PST (IML timestamps converted from UTC).


Confirmed memory operating in degraded Advanced ECC mode and RAID write cache disabled due to failed storage battery.


Correlated hardware degradation with reported hot server room conditions and loud fan noise, indicating thermal stress as contributing factor.


Confirmed degraded hardware state is causing VM performance degradation and user slowness.


Identified required hardware replacements: failed DIMM (P1 DIMM 1) and Smart Storage Battery.


Documented findings and prepared RCA and remediation recommendations.

TimeEntry.Title: InformXL
TimeEntry.Notes: Pon reported an issue with InformExcel not opening. Permission was obtained to access his computer, where it was confirmed that InformExcel was unresponsive and Adobe applications were also failing to launch. Both InformExcel and Adobe processes were forcefully ended. Available updates were checked, revealing one update for InformExcel and one for Adobe, which were installed. Adobe cache and temporary files were cleared, as excessive cache can cause application hangs, followed by repairing the Adobe installation. All temporary, prefetch, and hidden temp files were deleted. The system was restarted, and testing confirmed both applications were functioning properly per Pon’s confirmation successfully.

TimeEntry.Title: Veeam backups didn't run on scheduled time for BWH
TimeEntry.Notes: As reported by the team, the Veeam backups did not run at the scheduled time for BWH. I connected to the VBR server and found that some of the Veeam services were not starting properly after the upgrade to the latest version. The remaining services were set to Delayed Start.I then changed the affected services to Automatic Start and I launched the Veeam application and ran the backup job. The backup for two VMs initially failed, so I retried the jobs. Upon verification, both the backup jobs and the Offsite Backup Job completed successfully.

TimeEntry.Title: Proto - not previewing docs
TimeEntry.Notes: Investigated Proto / Newstar E-Sign document preview failing for a single workstation while functioning normally on other systems within the same network. Initial testing confirmed DNS resolution was correct and the target host resolved to the expected public IP. However, HTTPS connectivity attempts from the affected system timed out, while other machines received valid responses, indicating an endpoint-specific issue rather than a network-wide problem. Firewall and Group Policy settings were reviewed and confirmed to allow outbound traffic. Further analysis identified Cisco Umbrella and CrowdStrike Falcon Sensor as active security agents on the workstation. CrowdStrike events showed connection attempts without enforcement, while Umbrella was determined to be enforcing policy at the Secure Web Gateway / proxy layer. Domain allow and proxy bypass rules were verified within Umbrella to permit access to the Proto E-Sign service. After policy synchronization, HTTPS connectivity was restored and document preview functionality began working as expected.

TimeEntry.Title: One Drive Issues Still
TimeEntry.Notes:
As per handover worked with Microsoft Team with remote session and Here is the information:

The issue is caused by files/folders path being too long (more than 255 characters). Refer to this article: Restrictions and limitations in OneDrive and SharePoint - Microsoft Support. This limit is hard-coded and cannot be changed. The SharePoint that are causing the issue are the Documents - Project and Shared - Project


When OneDrive tries to sync the SharePoint site shortcut, it will sync the whole site at once. So, even if only one file has the long path, it will cause issue for the whole sync operation. It might be stopped or stuck in syncing status

I would advise 2 solutions for you:

Remove the SharePoint shortcut from your OneDrive: This will stop syncing the SharePoint files/folders with long paths and stop the error. You can still access the SharePoint files from Teams or SharePoint Online. This will temporarily fix the issue for local OneDrive. In the future, if your files/folders path exceeds 400 characters, it will cause issue for the SharePoint site.

Change the files/folders name to be shorter: This will allow you to add shortcut to your OneDrive. This requires a lot of manual work. You would need to change the structure of the SharePoint but it is the more stable way to deal with the issue.

TimeEntry.Title: Re: InformXL - Message error
TimeEntry.Notes:
• Connected to Pon's computer via remote support to investigate the informXL issue.

  • Uninstalled the existing version of informXL:

    • Opened the Control Panel and went to Programs and Features.

    • Found the existing informXL Analyzer Ribbon and uninstalled it.

  • Deleted any leftover files:

    • Ensured that no leftover files were present in the directory file://N:/informXL/User Setup and Documentation/Apps/Ribbon/ or similar directories.

    • Manually deleted any files or folders from the installation path mentioned in the error message.

  • Reinstalled the new version of informXL:

    • Ran the installer for the new informXL Ribbon add-in after uninstalling the old version.

    • The installation was successful without any conflicts.

  • Verified the add-in in Excel:

    • Opened Excel, went to File -> Options -> Add-ins, and ensured the informXL Ribbon COM Add-in was enabled.

    • Restarted Excel after confirming the add-in was enabled.

  • Informed the client that the issue was resolved and advised them to contact us if the issue persists or if further assistance is needed.

TimeEntry.Title: Re: Immediate Attention Required – ESXi Host Reboot Issue
TimeEntry.Notes:
Ticket Summary: HQ-ESXi Power-On, Memory, and Battery Issues

Issue:

  • ESXi host at HQ was experiencing issues related to memory and battery after being powered on.

  • The memory error persisted even after troubleshooting.

Actions Taken:

  1. Collaborated with Team:

    • Worked with Gurdeep, Sai, and Rishad to troubleshoot the issues.

    • Requested Rishad to power on the ESXi host at HQ.

    • Upon powering on, verified the host booted up successfully.

    • Assigned the management IP and ILO to the host for remote management and verified it was up and running.

  2. Memory and Battery Issues:

    • After booting, identified ongoing issues with memory and battery on the ESXi host.

    • Requested Rishad to power off the host to replace the faulty memory.

    • After replacement, the host displayed a different error, indicating a potential issue with the replacement memory or configuration.

    • Returned the original memory to its previous state and attempted to power the host back on.

Next Steps:

  • Further diagnose the memory error and verify whether the replacement was compatible.

  • Check if the battery issue is linked to BIOS/firmware settings or hardware.

  • Plan to power off the host again and attempt another memory replacement or consult with hardware support for a resolution.

TimeEntry.Title: BWH Server Issue
TimeEntry.Notes: The server unexpectedly rebooted during the night, and now ESXi is failing to start, causing all virtual machines to be offline. I checked the physical connections and attempted to identify the root cause. The server is an HPE ProLiant ML350 Gen9. I accessed the system through iLO to review the logs and attempted a soft reboot, but it was unsuccessful.

TimeEntry.Title: BWH Server Issue
TimeEntry.Notes: After attempting another reboot, the server failed to restart automatically. Having exhausted all other troubleshooting options, we opted to inspect the server and its internal hardware. As a result, the server was transported to Technijian HQ to safeguard the virtual machines (VMs) hosted within it. Alternatively, we can transfer them to a loaner server if required

TimeEntry.Title: BWH Server Issue
TimeEntry.Notes: We successfully rebooted the server on this attempt. Upon logging in, we promptly updated the IP address for the iLO interface and adjusted the ESXi IP to ensure compatibility with the Technijian network. We then inspected all adapters, identified any hardware errors, and performed necessary troubleshooting. The majority of the virtual machines (VMs) are now secure, and we have verified that each one is functioning properly.

TimeEntry.Title: BWH Server Issue
TimeEntry.Notes: We updated the BIOS to the latest version and confirmed that all server operations were proceeding smoothly. We implemented a minor modification by disabling the automatic shutdown feature triggered by high room temperatures. After a comprehensive review, the server was relocated to BWH and successfully installed there. It functioned as expected, with all components coming online. However, one virtual machine (VM) experienced an issue that impacted the Newstar application, which we addressed through targeted troubleshooting efforts. We remain uncertain whether the problem stems from Bluestar or our side, so we intend to observe the situation until Monday while assigning team members to continue investigating.

TimeEntry.Title: BWH Server Issue
TimeEntry.Notes: Worked on Troubleshooting issue with Newstar application and some internet issues in VM.

TimeEntry.Title: BWH Server Issue
TimeEntry.Notes: I coordinated with the India team on the troubleshooting progress and continued working on the issue. I verified that all VMs are connected to the correct networks, and I have now handed the case over to Rishad.

TimeEntry.Title: Server down
TimeEntry.Notes:
we noticed that the ProLiant ML350 Gen9 server was down. The outage appears to have been triggered by a power outage. System logs showed multiple unexpected server resets. The server failed to recover and displayed a Pre-Boot UEFI Firmware Error on boot.

As a result, ESXi was down and all virtual machines (VMs) were unavailable, including Active Directory, which caused network authentication and web access issues.

 

Saturday (1/24/2026)

  • Verified network connectivity; no web or AD access was available.
  • Checked the server through iLO, which showed that memory modules were not being detected.
  • Performed a soft reboot followed by a hard reboot.
  • During boot, the system initialized the QPI link and memory but consistently froze at 24%.
  • Multiple boot attempts failed to progress past 24%.

Due to continued failures, the decision was made to move the server to the test lab. A loaner server was arranged in case recovery was unsuccessful, with plans to restore VMs from backups if needed.

TimeEntry.Title: Server down
TimeEntry.Notes:
• Multiple boot attempts failed to progress past 24%.

Due to continued failures, the decision was made to move the server to the test lab. A loaner server was arranged in case recovery was unsuccessful, with plans to restore VMs from backups if needed.

TimeEntry.Title: Server down
TimeEntry.Notes:
LAB (Technijian)

  • After leaving the server powered off for several hours and booting it without Ethernet cables connected, the server booted successfully.
  • Network cables were reconnected and the server powered on again successfully.
  • Assigned an IP address to iLO and reviewed logs.
  • Logs showed high temperature events, which caused the server to shut down automatically.
  • Verified all VMs were operational with no data loss or VM-level errors.
  • Cleaned the server and internal fans.
  • Ran the server overnight for stability testing.

TimeEntry.Title: Server down
TimeEntry.Notes:
Sunday(1/25/26)

  • Checked logs the next day; no errors were reported and system stability was confirmed.
  • Noted that high-temperature automatic shutdown was enabled.
  • Disabled the shutdown setting and increased the alert temperature threshold.
  • Updated the server BIOS to the latest version.
  • Reinstalled the server at the BWH data center.
  • Server powered on successfully; VMs loaded normally.
  • Assigned iLO IP address and verified access.

One VM experienced an RDP issue, which was troubleshooted and resolved. Testing confirmed normal operation. Dave was informed.

TimeEntry.Title: Re: LIVE to Proto copy needed
TimeEntry.Notes:
Ticket Summary:

Issue:
Collaborated with Gurdeep to copy over data from the New Start database to Enterprise and Newstar Sales to Proto.

Action Taken:

  • Successfully updated and copied the data from the New Start database to Enterprise.

  • Transferred Newstar Sales data to Proto.

  • Completed the necessary updates to ensure proper data migration.

TimeEntry.Title: Re: LIVE to Proto copy needed
TimeEntry.Notes:
The task began with preparing for the DBCopy process, which involved copying the HBLive database to HBProto for the Proto environment. Initially, I reviewed the datasets and products required for the copy process. The Newstar Sales (NSS) dataset was identified for copying from SPLive to SPProto, which was included in the copy configuration.

I ensured that the destination HBProto database was stopped and ready for the restoration process. After configuring the source and target datasets in CHSOEControl, I confirmed that the necessary backup directories and paths were correctly set up. The DBCopy process was initiated after verifying that the datasets were ready for migration.

During the process, the system verified database compatibility, performed backups of both HBLive and SPLive SQL databases, and proceeded with restoring these backups to HBProto and SPProto. The backup was completed successfully, and SQL server settings were adjusted to ensure the target databases could accept the copied data.

Once the data restoration was complete, the DBCopy cleanup scripts were executed on the destination databases to finalize the copy process. The HBProto and SPProto databases were then brought back online, and the multi-user mode was set up for both.

However, during the copy process, Enterprise could not be found or selected in the system. I checked the configuration settings, but it was not listed as expected. I noted this issue and suggested verifying from the user’s end to determine if Enterprise is part of another dataset or requires further setup. If it's still unavailable, I recommended reaching out to Newstar Support to assist with its inclusion in the copy process.

The process was concluded successfully, with the HBProtoS and SPProto databases being fully restored and operational. The system confirmed that NSS was copied successfully, and all necessary backups were completed.

The task is now complete, and confirmation is awaited regarding the missing Enterprise dataset.

TimeEntry.Title: NewStar Setup and VPN Login Verification for BRANDYWINEDEV\MWilliams
TimeEntry.Notes:
Ticket Summary: NewStar Setup and Login Issue Resolution

Task: Resolve NewStar application setup and login issue for the user.

Issue: The user was facing difficulties with setting up NewStar and logging into the system.

Steps Taken:

  1. Pre-requisite Installations: Ensured all necessary applications were installed on the user's system before proceeding with the setup.

  2. VPN Connection: Verified that the user was able to connect to the system via VPN.

  3. NewStar Setup: Installed and configured NewStar on the user's system.

  4. Drive Mapping: Mapped the necessary drives and granted the user access.

  5. Login Verification: Successfully verified the user's ability to log in and access the required work resources.

  6. Testing: After the setup, the user was able to log in successfully and add work resources.
